Denial of service (DoS) attacks are often discussed in the context of cybersecurity, where understanding how they work is crucial for defending against them. A DoS attack is an attempt to make a computer or network resource unavailable of its intended users by overwhelming the target or its surrounding infrastructure with a flood of internet traffic. To defend against such attacks, organizations use various techniques, such as firewalls, rate limiting, and traffic filtering, to identify and block malicious traffic before it reaches the target. It is important to note that engaging in or facilitating a denial of service attack is illegal in many jurisdictions. The consequences of such actions can be severe and far-reaching.
